title: Acknowledgements, Delivery Tags, Redelivery, and Effect Ownership · Contract Lens: Redelivered Evidence Limit
summary: A handler acknowledges on a different channel, auto-acks before validation, nacks every error with requeue, treats redelivered false as first-ever processing, and double-acks after a timeout race. Planned artifact: Add and verify the redelivered evidence limit increment in delivery ownership and acknowledgement decision table. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use redelivered and delivery-count evidence as hints while relying on stable application identity and durable deduplication for correctness.
Transfer ownership only after the intended durable effect, transactional inbox record, or safely repeatable handoff succeeds.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Redelivered Evidence Limit · Theory Redelivered Evidence Limit
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 1514 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to enforce same-generation, exactly-one terminal acknowledgement after durable effect; bound payload, buffer, and retained-ledger bytes; then add one subscriber responsibility without changing publisher intent. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use redelivered and delivery-count evidence as hints while relying on stable application identity and durable deduplication for correctness.
